title: Pachystrobilus involucratus
taxonomy: Pachystrobilus involucratus is classified as a genus within the family Podocarpaceae, which is a group of coniferous plants. This genus is native to the subtropical regions of East Asia. The classification of Pachystrobilus involucratus is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae and the division Pinophyta. It belongs to the class Pinopsida and the order Pinales. The family Podocarpaceae comprises about 17 genera of coniferous plants, with Pachystrobilus involucratus being one of them.

intro: Pachystrobilus involucratus, also known as the Chinese fir or Pachystrobilus, is a type of coniferous tree native to China, belonging to the family Pinaceae. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are associated with its potential health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving cough, and treating respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Pachystrobilus involucratus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and teas, as well as being dried and used in the form of powder or granules.
